calendar.matters:
PETITIONER INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION OF PETITIONER INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY TO OBTAIN CELLULAR PHONE RECORDS FROM METROPCS BY T-MOBILE OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO ENFORCE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Mar-26-2024 Off Calendar

Motion Of Petitioner Infinity Insurance Company To Obtain Cellular Phone Records From Metropcs By T-Mobile Or Alternatively, To Enforce Deposition Subpoena; Declaration Of Jonathan H. Colman In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER INFINITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion Of Petitioner Infinity Insurance Company To Obtain Cellular Phone Records From Metropcs By T-Mobile Or Alternatively, To Enforce Deposition Subpoena

Respondent Maria Serrano, an insured of the petitioner Infinity Insurance Company, has filed a claim under her policy in connection with the theft of her insured automobile. As part of its processing of the claim, petitioner sought production of cellphone records for respondent and a third-party. Those records are maintained by T-Mobile which declined to produce the cellphone records due to its interpretation of governing law that requires a court order in addition to consents signed by the relevant cellphone account holders. Petitioner secured the required consents, and it now seeks issuance of a court order to satisfy T-Mobile's demands. There is no opposition to petitioner's request for a court order directed to T-Mobile.

For good cause shown, petitioner's motion for issuance of a court order directed to T-Mobile is GRANTED. The court file references a form of proposed order, but the Court was unable to locate that form. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order, in word format, Judge Pro Tem at psw@hassard.com for review and execution.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
